summary: 波普尔是20世纪最重要的科学哲学家之一，以可证伪性理论闻名。他建立了批判理性主义哲学体系，反对逻辑实证主义，为科学方法论和开放社会理论奠定基础。

core_themes.theme: 可证伪性作为科学划界标准
core_themes.description: 波普尔提出，科学的本质特征不是能够被证实，而是能够被证伪。一个理论如果不能在原则被证伪，它就不是科学理论。这一标准区分了科学与非科学（如精神分析、马克思主义历史决定论）。

quotes.text: Einstein's theory was highly 'risky', in the sense that it was possible to deduce consequences from it which were, in the light of the then dominant Newtonian physics, highly improbable... and which would, if they turned out to be false, falsify the whole theory, nothing could, even in principle, falsify psychoanalytic theories. They were, Popper argues, 'simply non-testable, irrefutable. There was no conceivable human behaviour which could contradict them'

Real support can be obtained only from observations undertaken as tests (by 'attempted refutations'); and for this purpose criteria of refutation have to be laid down beforehand: it must be agreed which observable situations, if actually observed, mean that the theory is refuted.

core_themes.description: 波普尔的哲学核心是批判理性主义——知识通过猜测与反驳而增长，理性即是对批评的开放。不存在最终正当化的知识，所有理论都是可错的（fallible）。

quotes.text: I approached the problem of induction through Hume. Hume, I felt, was perfectly right in pointing out that induction cannot be logically justified.
There is no logical path leading to [the highly universal laws of science]. They can only be reached by intuition, based upon something like an intellectual love of the objects of experience.

core_themes.description: 波普尔是坚定的可错论者，认为所有知识本质上都是试探性的、假设性的。科学进步不是积累确定的真理，而是在猜测与反驳中逼近真理（逼真性）。

quotes.text: Logically, no number of positive outcomes at the level of experimental testing can confirm a scientific theory, but a single counterexample is logically decisive; it shows the theory, from which the implication is derived, to be false.
The more improbable a theory is the better it is scientifically, because the probability and informative content of a theory vary inversely.

core_themes.theme: 开放社会与反极权主义
core_themes.description: 波普尔是民主自由的坚定捍卫者，提出开放社会概念——尊重个人权利、政策基于最佳证据、始终接受公众监督、理性讨论解决分歧。他对极权主义持坚决反对态度。

quotes.text: an association of free individuals respecting each other's rights within the framework of mutual protection supplied by the state, and achieving, through the making of responsible, rational decisions, a growing measure of humane and enlightened life.
He was a dogged opponent of totalitarianism, communism, nationalism, fascism, historicism, and other kinds of (in Popper's view) reactionary and irrational ideas

related_works: The Open Society and Its Enemies (1945)

core_themes.description: 波普尔提出客观知识世界（世界3）独立存在，包括语言、故事、宗教神话、科学理论和艺术作品。世界3影响世界1（物理世界），知识是人类文化的客观积累。

quotes.text: The world of the products of the human mind, such as languages; tales and stories and religious myths; scientific conjectures or theories, and mathematical constructions; songs and symphonies; paintings and sculptures.
…my thesis is that our world 3 theories and our world 3 plans causally influence the physical objects of world 1; that they have a causal action upon world 1.

core_themes.description: 波普尔批判历史决定论，认为历史无法被科学预测。因为知识的增长本身就是历史变化的因，而我们在事先无法知道未来将获得什么知识。历史主义是极权主义的思想根源。

quotes.text: the dream of prophecy, the idea that we can know what the future has in store for us, and that we can profit from such knowledge by adjusting our policy to it.
no society can predict, scientifically, its own future states of knowledge
quotes.source: The Open Society and Its Enemies (1945)

related_works: The Open Society and Its Enemies (1945)

evaluation.confidence_rationale: 波普尔著作丰富，核心思想清晰可追溯，引语来源可靠。主要著作均有明确引用。其思想影响深远且被学界广泛讨论。
evaluation.completeness: 完整覆盖了波普尔的六个核心思想维度，每个主题都有至少2处原文引语支持。
key_sources: Stanford Encyclopedia of Philosophy - Karl Popper
The Open Society and Its Enemies (1945)
